# 'gs continue' on the merge restack path, while conflicts are still
# unresolved, fails and keeps the merge in progress until the conflict
# is actually resolved and staged.

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2024-05-27T18:24:42Z'

mkdir repo
cd repo
git init
git add init.txt
git commit -m 'Initial commit'
gs repo init

# use the merge method for restacks.
git config spice.restack.method merge

# create a feature branch that modifies init.
cp $WORK/extra/init.feature.txt init.txt
git add init.txt
gs bc -m feature

# go back to main and modify init in a conflicting way.
gs trunk
cp $WORK/extra/init.new.txt init.txt
git add init.txt
git commit -m 'Change init'

gs up
stderr 'feature: needs to be restacked'

# restack the feature branch: the merge conflicts.
! gs branch restack
stderr 'There was a conflict while merging'
stderr '  gs continue'
stderr '  gs abort'

# a merge is in progress.
exists .git/MERGE_HEAD

# run 'gs continue' WITHOUT resolving the conflict: it must fail,
# report that conflicts remain, and keep the merge in progress.
env EDITOR=true
! gs continue
stderr 'There are more conflicts to resolve'
exists .git/MERGE_HEAD

# now actually resolve the conflict, stage it, and continue.
cp $WORK/extra/init.resolved.txt init.txt
git add init.txt
gs continue

# the merge is finished.
! exists .git/MERGE_HEAD
! stderr 'There are more conflicts to resolve'

# verify resolved content and a merge commit on the branch.
cmp init.txt $WORK/extra/init.resolved.txt
git graph --branches
cmp stdout $WORK/golden/graph.txt

-- repo/init.txt --
initial init

-- extra/init.new.txt --
changed init

-- extra/init.feature.txt --
feature's init

-- extra/init.resolved.txt --
updated init

-- golden/graph.txt --
*   2ac3f2c (HEAD -> feature) Merge branch 'main' into feature
|\  
| * 57ab3b0 (main) Change init
* | 10ab8d9 feature
|/  
* d692027 Initial commit
